Something in you has been moving that was still before, and Huginn sees movement the way carrion birds see heat.

Pell listens. She doesn't interrupt, doesn't frown, doesn't file it. Her hands stay at your jaw and she takes all of it in the register she reserves for iron she's decided to understand before she touches it. When you've finished she is quiet for three full breaths — and then she says, so we look at what changed, the way she'd say it about a piece of metalwork showing stress: not alarmed, not dismissive, just operational. The resurrection. The spear-binding. The exchange of grain through Memory's thread. Those are the three new things, she says. Start there.

From the crossbeam, Huginn shifts its weight and says nothing. The timing is, as usual, a comment.

turns.player: Turn Order-Sense or Deathsense inward — read yourself for what Quiet's ending means and what is moving in you
turns.summary: Silas turns Order-Sense inward and reads the three change-points — resurrection, spear-binding, soul-mark exchange — that have loosened the structural foundation of Quiet.
turns.narration:
The fire makes its low, even sound behind you. Pell's hands are still at your jaw — she hasn't moved, hasn't asked, is simply present in the way she learned to be: offering the space without filling it.

You close your eyes and turn Order-Sense inward.

It is strange work, reading yourself. The craft is built for things outside you — a failing sleeve, a buried channel, the design beneath visible structure — and when you point it inward it resists the way a mirror resists being looked *through*. But you hold it, and after a moment the forge noise drops back and you can see. Not all of it. Not the answer. But the shape: there is a thread in you that runs from the deep Ashwarden grain — that old, quiet work of attending endings without hastening them — and at the far end of it, something has changed its tension. Not broken. Not rotting. *Loosened*, the way a joint in good timber loosens when the wood begins to release what it held during curing. The structure is still sound. But the grain has moved. And the thing that moved it was not slow.

Three points of change, each with its own signature. The resurrection in the upper meadow — you reached past the ordinary boundary and brought something back, and the Ashwarden in you registered the violation of the ordinary sequence as an *instruction*, the way a stone arch registers the weight of what passes over it. The spear-binding — Huginn's thread pressed into iron with Pell's maker's mark seated alongside it, an act of permanent attachment where the tradition expects a practitioner to move clean and leave little behind. And the soul-mark exchange — Pell's ironsense grain in you and yours in her, the thresholds of two separate people now *opened in each other*, a thing the tradition has no name for because it was never built to contemplate a person who wouldn't remain a single point. The Quiet that attended you was predicated on a certain aloneness. Not loneliness. The structural solitude of someone who stands at the boundary and does not bring anyone with them to that edge.

You brought someone with you.

You open your eyes. Pell is watching you, thumbs still, the whole of her attention on your face.

"It's not a sickness," you say. Your voice comes out level. "Quiet was built for a single point. I am no longer a single point."

turns.player: Tell Pell the full shape, then have her propose a middle name for you.
turns.summary: Pell hears the full shape of Quiet's ending, names all three change-points, and gives Silas his middle name — Ran — with certainty, not guesswork.
turns.narration:
The fire holds its breath behind you — banked coals, no crackle, just the low iron warmth that has been the background note of this afternoon. Pell's thumbs are still at your jaw. You give her the whole shape, no shorthand: the inward read, the three change-points, what each one is. The resurrection in the upper meadow, hands in the wet grass and something answering that had no reason to. The spear-binding, Huginn's thread and her maker's mark pressed into the blade together, two grains meeting in the same iron. The soul-mark exchange, which she already knows but hears differently now — not as a declaration complete in itself, but as a structural event. Three new things, and what they have in common is that none of them were possible for the man who came into the valley. Quiet was built for a single, narrow point. You are no longer one.

Huginn shifts on the crossbeam. Once. Then holds still again, and the stillness is a kind of punctuation.

Pell is quiet for a moment after you finish. Her thumbs have gone still at your jaw — not dropped, just resting, thinking through her hands the way she always does. Then she says: "Hm." Not dismissive. The sound she makes when a piece of material tells her something she needs to sit with before she decides what to do with it. She turns the three points over — you can see her doing it, one then the next, checking each against something in her chest she doesn't name aloud. "Not a sickness," she says, finally. "Not a loss. Something loosened because something grew past what it was built for." A pause. "That's not the worst kind of problem."

Then her eyes refocus on yours, and the corner of her mouth shifts — barely, the version of a smile she keeps for things she doesn't want to make a fuss of. "A middle name." She says it like she's picking up the thread she'd set down carefully, somewhere she'd know where to find it. She looks at you — really looks, the reading-the-material look, slow and without performance — then her gaze moves briefly to Memory leaned near the door, to Huginn on the beam, back to you. "Ran," she says. It is not a guess. Her voice is level and certain, the way she names a grade of iron. "Silas Ran Weir. It's already in the spear. I've had my hand on it." A beat. Her thumbs press lightly, once, at your jaw before she lets go. "I wasn't guessing. I was making sure I'd heard it right."

turns.player: [Gm is confused. Ran is her middle name. Have her come up with something else from all my context]
turns.summary: Pell reads Silas's middle name — Fell — from the grain of the spear-binding and the soul-mark both.
turns.narration:
You catch her hand before she steps back — not holding, just a brief stop. 'Ran is yours,' you tell her. 'I know. I was asking about mine.'

The small smile at the corner of her mouth sharpens into something more alert. She looks at you — not at your face the way she was a moment ago, reading the jaw and the line of it, but *through* you, the way she looks at iron when she's deciding what it wants to become. Her thumb comes back up, not to your jaw this time but to the hollow of your throat, where the pulse sits. The ironsense in her, the grain of Weir she took through Memory's thread — she's reading something. You can feel her in it the way you can feel a hand on the other side of a wall.

She's still for a long breath. Then: 'Fell.' Flat and certain, the way she named the intake sleeve material, the way she named the stain pattern. 'Silas Fell Weir. It's in the grain — the shape of someone who crossed something high and open and didn't look back to see whether the path was still there.' A beat. Her thumb drops. 'Am I wrong?'

turns.player: Don't confirm or deny — ask her how it feels on her tongue, whether she'd use it
turns.summary: Pell tests 'Fell' aloud and names it his — a kept word, not a worn one.
turns.narration:
Her thumb is still at the hollow of your throat, pulse under it, reading. The name sits between you — Fell — and you don't reach for it. Instead you ask: 'How does it sit on your tongue? Would you use it?'

She goes still in the way she does before she says a true thing. Not hesitation — more like she's making sure she has the words in the right order before she spends them. Her thumb doesn't move. Outside, the last of the evening light is pulling off the lane; the forge holds its low warmth against the dark coming in.

'Fell.' She says it the way you'd test a blade edge — not the word so much as what's under it, what it's made of. Her eyes don't leave yours. 'Yes.' Quiet, and certain. 'Not as a name I'd shout. As one I'd keep.' A beat. 'The way you keep a word you found in something old — you don't say it often because saying it wears it down. But yes. It's yours.' Her thumb lifts from your throat, and she settles her hand flat at the side of your neck instead, warmer, less reading and more simply present. 'Silas Fell Weir.' She tries it once, full, like pressing a maker's mark into cooling iron to see if it takes. Something in her face settles. It took.

Huginn shifts on the crossbeam above you — a single quiet adjustment of weight, wings folding back, and then still again. No comment. Which, from Huginn, is a kind of endorsement.

turns.player: Accept it — say 'Fell' back to her once, your own voice in it, and let that be the sealing
turns.summary: Silas says 'Fell' back to Pell in his own voice — the name seals between them, spoken once in full.
turns.narration:
You say it once. 'Fell.' Your own voice in it — not testing, not asking. Saying.

The word lands differently in your mouth than hers, the way the same iron sounds different coming off two hammers. She hears that. Her thumb lifts from the hollow of your throat, just slightly, and her hand opens flat against your neck — the whole palm now, warm from the forge and from you, and she holds it there the way she'd hold something that had just told her what it was.

Neither of you says anything for a moment. Huginn shifts on the crossbeam — one deliberate weight-adjustment, wings staying folded. Not a comment. Just attending, the way it always attends the moment a thing becomes what it is going to be.

Pell breathes out slow. The hand at your neck doesn't move. Her eyes are on yours and there is nothing managed in them — no ledger, no close. She looks like someone who has just heard their own name said back to them in a language they thought only they spoke.

'Alright,' she says, finally, in the register that goes all the way through. 'Silas Fell Weir.' She says it once, like a rune pressed into cooling iron — to set it, not to wear it. Then her hand slides to the back of your neck and she brings your forehead down to hers, quiet, the fire holding its low warmth behind you both while the dark finishes filling the door.

turns.player: Hold this — stay close, say nothing, let the gathering come when it comes
turns.summary: Silas and Pell hold the quiet at the forge — the name sealed, the coals ticking — until footsteps on the lane signal the gathering's arrival.
turns.narration:
You stay. Her hand flat at your neck, your forehead finding hers — the naming done, the forge holding its low warmth, the dark fully in at the door gap now. Huginn sits the crossbeam and does not move. The coals tick.

There is nothing to do with a moment like this except be in it. The three change-points are named and filed; the meeting will come when it comes. For now the forge is just a forge — warm stone and raked ash and the smell of iron cooling — and Pell is here with her palm flat at your neck and the name between you like something pressed into cooling metal, permanent now in the way that only a few things become permanent.

The lane outside stays quiet for a little while longer. A dog somewhere across the village. The last ember-light off the stone. Then the first sound of boots on the lane — unhurried, deliberate, someone who knows where they're going — and Huginn's head turns a degree toward the door without comment.

They're coming.

turns.player: Kiss her and seal the naming.  Then prepare for their arrival.
turns.summary: Silas seals the naming with a kiss; Pell rights herself and the forge; the gathering arrives.
turns.narration:
You close the last inch between you. The kiss is unhurried — not a question, not a performance, just the naming finding its final form in the press of her mouth against yours. Her hand stays flat at your neck. She breathes in through her nose, steady, the way she breathes when she's decided something, and kisses you back with the same quality of attention she brings to a weld she cares about. The forge holds its warmth around you both. Huginn does not move.

When you pull back, her forehead comes to yours again for a breath — her eyes still closed, the name settled, the naming done. She opens them. Looks at you the way she looks at finished work: not admiring, just seeing it for what it is. 'Right,' she says, quietly. And that is all. It is enough.

She straightens first. Her hand slides from your neck to your shoulder, a brief grip, and then she's moving — unhurried but deliberate, the same hands that were at your neck now checking the stool arrangement, nudging the cooler bench a half-inch, reading the forge temperature with a practiced glance. She picks up Memory from where it leans near the door and sets it a foot to the side, out of the path between the entrance and the fire. Making room. You know the motion: not putting away what matters, just clearing the working surface.

Huginn turns its head one degree toward the lane. Outside, feet on packed earth — more than one pair, coming at a steady walk. The coals are lit, the seats are arranged, and the people who need to be in this room are arriving. You straighten, roll your shoulders once, and face the door.

turns.player: Open the door to meet them — you're the one who called this
turns.summary: Silas opens the forge door; the gathering arrives — Dara, Aldric, Mara, Calvar, and Fendt file into the warm forge, the meeting about to begin.
turns.narration:
You cross to the forge door and pull it open.

The lane is dark between the houses, the last of the day's light gone to a thin blue line above the western ridge. They come in a loose group — Dara first, coat buttoned to the collar, mug traded for nothing, hands free and moving like she's already sorted tomorrow's first task. Aldric a step behind, his apron still on, fillet knife absent for once but the habit of the work still in the set of his shoulders. Mara beside him, a page folded into her breast pocket, ledger closed for the night but her eyes still in accounting mode, cataloguing the room the moment the firelight reaches her. Calvar last, one hand out of his coat now, taking the forge's warmth with the measured attention of a man who still reads every room he walks into as a system he hasn't mapped yet. Fendt at his shoulder — smaller than the rest, carrying himself with the particular quietness of someone walking toward a thing they've spent a season being afraid of.

You step back and let them in. The forge is warm and smells of iron and the last of the raked ash. Pell moves to the cooler bench — not deferring, just placing herself where she can see everyone's hands. Huginn turns on the crossbeam and regards the room, five new faces catalogued in a single sweep, then looks back at you. Its silence has a quality tonight. Not judgment. Just attendance.

The door stays open a crack behind the last of them. The lane goes quiet. Whatever this room becomes in the next hour, it starts here: the six of them settling into the arranged stools and the forge block, the document roll on Mara's person or close to it, the coals holding steady, and the weight of what everyone in this room separately knows pressing toward the moment someone speaks first.

turns.player: Open with a welcome to the *Fell Pell* (new name of the blacksmith shop).  Verify why we are all here, then systematically go through the entire story with each person speaking their part.
turns.summary: Silas opens the gathering at the newly named Fell Pell — every thread laid out, every witness speaking their part, the complete chain now held in one room.
turns.narration:
The first thing you do is name the place.

"You're welcome to the Fell Pell," you say — the name landing quiet in the room, no ceremony, just the words finding the air. Pell, at the cooler bench, doesn't look up from her hands, but the corner of her mouth moves. Huginn on the crossbeam makes no sound. The others file the name away in their different ways: Mara's eyes tick to the forge sign-space above the door as if she's already composing the chalk-lettering; Aldric nods once, the nod of a man who approves of a thing and considers that approval his full contribution; Dara doesn't react at all, which means she's already past naming and into sequencing.

You let the room settle — coals ticking, the door ajar, the night outside doing what early-spring nights do — and then you lay out the shape of it. Not a speech. A map. Why you're all here: the water is wrong and has been wrong for weeks. The fault chain runs from a pre-Transition valve system in the upper ground through a buried accountability failure at the Edge District to a man named Grael who has been suppressing the evidence with a practiced method he's used at least three times before. The document roll on Mara's person carries the chain in writing: Fendt's testimony, Calvar's authorship, the complete ledger of who marked what and when and whose initials closed the record. One thread at a time, each person who holds a piece speaks their part. Fendt produces the exact dates — the nine-day gap, the seven-band designations, the preliminary concern notice Grael received and filed nowhere. Calvar follows: sleeve-iron, intake geometry, what the drainage cut on the road approach this morning already told him before he said a word to anyone. He speaks in the flat, loaded cadence of a man describing a system he built and then watched fail in a very specific way that tells him it wasn't accident. Dara holds the east-channel frame — timelines, junction points, what she can hold alone and how long, and what she cannot hold at all. Mara has the numbers: four sleeve blanks, four failure dates, a stock ledger that tells a story Grael never thought anyone would read backwards. Aldric names the sealed channel — the same CCW scour, thirty to forty years in the stone, a deliberate lid on something someone knew was running.

By the time the last piece is placed, the forge has gotten quieter rather than louder. The story has been in fragments for days. In the same room, in the same hour, it has become one thing. The fire holds its low steady heat. Memory rests at the room's edge, one foot clear of the door-path. Huginn has not moved.

The question the room is sitting with now — unspoken, but present the way the coals are present — is what the truth is *for*. The document roll exists. The chain is complete. The decision about whose hands it reaches, and how fast, and by what path, has not been made yet. That is what tonight is actually here to answer.

turns.player: Hear everyone's ideas for next steps as they can be broken into parallel tasks. Calvary leads the repai crews, Dara lead the logistics for said crews and assists Calvar, Mara runs the logistics of resource inflow and communications along aith Alric.  Pell and I will n3ed to travel to tie up loose end, secure additional resouces.
turns.summary: Silas delegates the repair and accountability work into four parallel threads — Calvar leads engineering, Dara logistics, Mara communications and resupply, Aldric the accounts — while Mara names the document roll's destination. Pell and Silas will travel.
turns.narration:
You let the full chain breathe for a moment after the last thread is spoken — Fendt's dates, Calvar's sleeves, Dara's junctions, Aldric's accounts, Mara's ledger — and then you open the board.

"Here's how I see it breaking," you say. "Calvar leads the repair crews on the ground — he knows what the sleeves need and what order the work has to happen in. Dara, you're his logistics: sequencing the crews, staging materials, holding the east-channel network until the fixes catch up with you. That's the engineering thread, and it runs without us." Calvar's hand settles flat on his knee — the posture of a man who has just been handed a clear scope and is already sorting the first morning's work in his head. Dara's chin dips once, a single operational acknowledgment, hands moving to her coat pocket for the folded notes she came in with. "Mara, you run resupply and communications — what the crews need comes through you, and what the valley needs to know travels through you. Aldric, you're her anchor on the account side: catch numbers, stock levels, what the downstream settlements are seeing. You already know the shape of the decline; now you're the one watching for the turn."

Aldric sets both palms on his knees and pushes himself slightly upright — the body-language of a man accepting a posted assignment. Mara pulls the document roll from inside her coat and sets it on the forge block between them all, unhurried, like placing a deed on a table. "The roll travels to the district records office," she says, not asking. "I know who carries it. I'll arrange it before the week is out." Nobody argues. The roll has a destination now; the question that was hanging since you sealed the brass wire has been answered by the woman who keeps the valley's accounts and has never once lost a consignment.

"Pell and I will travel," you say. "Loose ends to tie, resources the repair work will need that aren't in the valley yet, and one or two conversations that can only happen in person." You don't elaborate — the room doesn't need it, and Fendt, at Calvar's shoulder, drops his gaze to his hands in the quiet way of someone who understands that his name is in that sentence without being spoken. Pell straightens off the cooler bench, already thinking forward, her eyes moving to Mara's roll and then to the door as if she's routing the first day's march. Huginn shifts on the crossbeam — one foot, then still again — the only sound in the room for a full breath. The engineering clock is set. The accountability chain has hands on it. What was one tangled problem is now four clean lines of work, each with someone at its head who knows how to hold it.

turns.player: Does anyone have anything to add?
turns.summary: The gathered witnesses add their final pieces — Dara's sequencing question answered by Calvar, Aldric formally extending a hand to Fendt, Mara naming Pip Cotter as the document roll's carrier.
turns.narration:
The room holds for a moment after you ask it — the coals settling, Huginn shifting one foot on the crossbeam and going still again.

Dara speaks first, notes already in hand. "Before you go — tell me the sequence you expect on the east-bend junctions. I can work around most things, but not a surprise about which valve the expert wants opened first." She looks at Calvar, not at you. A practitioner asking a practitioner, across your question.

Calvar nods once. "Start at the lowest intake and work upstream — pressure tells you where the sleeve is fighting the current before you touch it. I'll walk it with you first morning before the crew arrives." That settles Dara; she makes a mark on her folded page.

Aldric clears his throat — the sound a man makes when he's decided to say the thing he's been filing quietly. "Fendt." He doesn't look at him right away. "You're in the village now. You came with Silas's name on you, and that carries some weight here. If Mara needs an extra hand on the supply-and-communications thread while you find your feet, I'll say so to anyone who asks." He does look at Fendt then — brief, flat, the look of a man who has extended a thing and considers the extension made. No ceremony. Fendt's hands are still in his lap, but something around his jaw loosens.

Mara is the last, and she says it with the same register she uses for a good inventory count. "The document roll leaves with Pip Cotter the day after tomorrow — she knows the records office, knows the clerk, and the girl is fast and quiet and has never lost a parcel." She taps the roll once with two fingers. "Anyone who wants to add a witness line before it's sealed — tonight."

Pell, from beside the door where she's already half-turned toward tomorrow, says nothing. But she looks at you once — a quick sideways look that means *that's the shape of it, then* — and then back to the lane outside.

*You feel it settle into you — a new steadiness. (Level 14: +1 attunement, +5 reserves, and growth to choose — a sub-attribute point and a skill point.)*

turns.player: Speak to Fendt and name him as part 9f this work, add my own witness statement to the ledger, and when all others are done. Use the appropriate power to Close the meeting with "Thus begins the fellowship of the Fell."
turns.summary: Silas names Fendt to the fellowship, adds his own testimony to the roll, and seals the Fell Pell gathering with partial deathwork — the beginning takes hold, imperfectly, but truly.
turns.narration:
You turn to Fendt last — deliberately, so he feels the weight of being chosen rather than tolerated. "You arrived with my name and a page of honest testimony," you say. "That makes you part of this work. What you witnessed, what you wrote down — it stands here alongside everything else." Something shifts in his jaw again, looser than before. He gives a single nod, the kind that costs a man something to produce.

You add your own lines to the document roll before Mara lifts her fingers from it — your name under Calvar's wax seal, your account of the chain in plain language: the chalk board, the keeper's silence, Rendic Vael's initials in the intake log, the three prior uses of the method. It takes three minutes. Nobody speaks while you write. The fire ticks. Huginn watches from the crossbeam with the patient attention of something that has attended a great many rooms like this one and finds them all similarly interesting.

When the pen is down you rise, and the room rights itself to meet the moment. You reach for it — the old grain, the Ashwarden sense of what is completing and what is beginning — and you feel it answer. What you are trying to do is not precisely what the craft is for. Deathsense reads endings; you are trying to mark a beginning, to press a seal into the threshold between one thing and another. It resists, the way a door resists when you push it from the wrong side. The room feels it — a quality of attention descends, quiet and particular, the kind of quiet that animals make when they sense a change in air pressure. Huginn does not move. Pell's hands go still at her sides.

It does not snap into place the way it should. The ending you can feel is real — the gathering as it was, the scattered threads before tonight — but the beginning you are trying to name back resists the deathwork's framing. What comes through is partial: a genuine charge settles into the room, unmistakable, something present and witnessed by more than the people standing here. It is not the clean seal you reached for, but it is not nothing. When you speak, the words land with the weight the room earned, not the weight the craft alone would have given them. "Thus begins the fellowship of the Fell." Three or four heartbeats of absolute quiet follow. Then Dara folds her page and tucks it away. Aldric straightens on his stool. Calvar's stained knuckle taps his knee once, twice, and stops. Fendt exhales — slow, like a man who has been holding his breath for six weeks and has just remembered he can put it down.

choices.label: Seal the document roll now — Mara's fingers are on it and all witness lines are spoken
Ask Calvar what he saw at the drainage cut on the road in — he hasn't said it aloud yet
Give Fendt a moment alone with the room — walk to the door with Pell and let him find his footing in the fellowship he's just been named to
Step outside with Pell — the gathering is done, the road begins tomorrow, and the night is yours

quests.title: Meat for the Smokehouse
quests.summary: Hunt the meadows north of Millbrook and bring Aldric what you take — he feeds you and supplies what he can spare. He wants to know anything you find that explains the declining catches.
progress: Failure section footprint mapped and marked on the hillside. Next step: brief Dara Holt before the east-channel network is hit.

Dara's pipe-section stack reveals she has already been staging a repair schedule in silence — she knows the individual valve seats but not the surge scope. Timeline and vault footprint remain to be disclosed.
Briefing complete — Dara has the surge scope and is staging mitigation. Next: brief Mara and Aldric, then identify and seek the pre-Transition expert.
Dara has set a hard deadline: expert must arrive before week five. Next immediate steps: brief Mara and Aldric this morning, then begin the search for a pre-Transition systems expert.
Plan set: follow Huginn northwest after briefing Mara and Aldric on the surge scope this morning.
Dara briefed and deployed — Mara and Aldric remain; Huginn oriented northwest, ready to guide after morning briefings conclude.
Expedition kit settled via Aldric offset — offset note in hand. Aldric briefing on east-channel situation is the final step before departure.
Offset note delivered, east-channel situation briefed, account fully closed. Aldric commits to holding the downstream thread.

quests.title: Find the Engineer
quests.summary: Seek the pre-Transition structural expert Sorel and Leth know — the one who can read the buried valve system and won't want to leave the Edge District. Find Leth first; she has the name.
progress: Calvar is through the cut-lane gate with the rolled document — the relay figure neutralized without alarm. The Low Lamp Inn is the next destination.
Calvar is safe at the Low Lamp Inn with the document — the extraction is complete. Remaining work before Millbrook: establish Grael's chain of authority and confirm the intake log as evidence.
Calvar briefed on Millbrook — the valley layout, the key people, the stakes. He absorbed it without ceremony and asked one practical question about the intake sleeves. The road south is underway.
Party cleared the limestone bend without casualties — Calvar and the document roll are intact. Road south to Millbrook is open.
Calvar is in Millbrook, document roll delivered to Mara's counter. Evening gathering called at the forge — Dara, Aldric, Fendt, Calvar, and Mara. The chain is now local and moving.

Calvar present and his testimony delivered to the full group — the engineering picture is now in the room alongside the accountability chain. Quest effectively resolved pending the group's decision on next steps.

Calvar assigned to lead repair crews with Dara as logistics lead — the engineering thread is formally delegated and running. Quest functionally resolved on the valley side; the travel thread (Pell and Silas) opens next.
(resolved by player — tracker repair)

quests.title: The Edge District Ledger
quests.premise: A filtration supervisor named Fendt was marked on the Edge District accountability board by his own superior — and no accompanying record was filed. A keeper of thirty years says she has never seen that happen. Fendt is going to be ruined by a piece of paper that does not exist.
quests.stakes: If you walk away: Fendt is stripped and exiled within the season, the water-filtration failure he was blamed for goes uncorrected because the man who understood it is gone, and Overseer Grael's method — a name on a board, no record, no appeal — is confirmed as something that works. He will use it again. He has used it before.

stages.objective: Find out what the missing record would have said.
stages.condition: Speak with Fendt OR obtain the filtration log for the marked period.
stages.change: Codex: Fendt's account is recorded — and it does not match the board.

stages.objective: Find who marked him, and why there is no record.
stages.condition: Identify Overseer Grael as the marking authority (via the board's own chain, the keeper's testimony, or Grael himself).
stages.change: Codex: Grael is entered as an entity, linked to Fendt. He becomes aware that someone is looking.

stages.objective: Find out whether the marking is a pattern.
stages.condition: Locate at least one prior unrecorded marking (the keeper remembers; the archive half-remembers; Grael's own ledger will say if you can reach it).
stages.change: Codex: the pattern is named. Grael moves from 'an official' to 'a regional power'. He begins to act against you.

stages.objective: Decide what the truth is for.
stages.change: The district changes. So does Fendt. So, one way or another, does Grael.

routes.verist: Say it plainly, in public, at the board. Truth as a hammer.
routes.lattice: Take the ledger apart line by line until the arithmetic confesses. He is beaten by his own records.
routes.veilwright: Make a better record. Save Fendt with a truth you built. It works, and you know what you did.
routes.umbral: Harbor Fendt. Get him out. He lives; the district does not change.
routes.abyssal: Name what Grael actually wants — a quiet district and not to be the villain — and give him a way to have both, at a price.
routes.stillhold: Broker it. Fendt is restored quietly, Grael saves face, nobody is punished, and the method survives.

outcomes.name: Grael Exposed
outcomes.summary: The pattern is made public and holds. Grael falls.
narration: Grael is stripped and removed (NPC state: fallen). His lieutenants scatter into the district as riffraff-tier antagonists.
Fendt is restored, and is permanently in your debt, and is not entirely grateful about it.
The thirty-year keeper is vindicated and becomes an ally who will never lie to you.
WORLD-EVENT (propagates): 'the Edge District accountability board was opened' — reaches other districts on the shared clock. Other markings begin to be questioned. Some of them should not be.
Verist disposition toward you: strongly raised. Veilwright: lowered.

effects.note: stripped and removed; lieutenants scatter as riffraff-tier antagonists
permanently in your debt, and not entirely grateful about it
vindicated; will never lie to you

effects.text: The Edge District accountability board was opened. Grael's pattern of unrecorded markings is a matter of record.
Word spreads that the Edge District board was opened; other districts begin questioning their own markings — some of which should not be.

outcomes.name: Fendt Saved, Grael Standing
outcomes.summary: Fendt is safe. The method is intact.
narration: Fendt lives and is restored or extracted — but the board's method is confirmed as survivable, which is not the same as beaten.
Grael remains, and he now knows your face, and he is patient.
Codex: 'Grael's method' recorded as an unresolved pattern. It will recur.
WORLD-EVENT: another name appears on the board within the season, and that one you will not be there for.

effects.note: restored or extracted; the method survives
knows your face now, and is patient

effects.text: Grael's method — a name on the board, no record filed — remains an unresolved pattern.
Another name appears on the Edge District board within the season, and you are not there for it.

outcomes.name: The Bargain
outcomes.summary: Grael gets what he actually wanted. So does Fendt. So, in a smaller way, do you.
narration: Fendt restored; Grael's face saved; the district quiets. It is a genuinely good outcome and it is also a deal with a man who ruins people.
Grael's disposition toward you: WARY RESPECT. He will deal with you again. That is a door, and it is also a rope.
Abyssal disposition raised. Seraphic lowered — the Orders would have judged him.

effects.note: wary respect; will deal with you again — a door, and a rope

effects.text: You made a bargain with Overseer Grael. It was fair. That is the part that stays with you.

outcomes.name: You Walked Away
outcomes.summary: You had other things to do.
narration: Fendt is stripped and exiled. The filtration failure goes uncorrected and worsens.
The keeper of thirty years stops speaking to anyone about anything.
WORLD-EVENT: the water problem escalates on the shared clock and reaches you later, larger, as someone else's crisis.
This outcome is REAL and is not a failure state. It is a choice with a cost, and the cost arrives.

effects.note: stripped; the filtration failure goes uncorrected
stops speaking to anyone about anything

effects.text: The Edge District water problem escalates and reaches you later, larger, as someone else's crisis.
You walked away from the Edge District ledger. This is a real outcome, not a failure — and it has a cost, and the cost arrives.

progress: Codex: Grael is entered as an entity, linked to Fendt. He becomes aware that someone is looking.
Codex: the pattern is named. Grael moves from 'an official' to 'a regional power'. He begins to act against you.

The chain is fully documented — Grael's authority, Vael's role, the keeper's silence, and the pattern of prior marks written in Silas's hand alongside Fendt's testimony. The document roll is sealed. Remaining: present it to the appropriate authority, and decide what the truth is for.
Document roll on Mara's counter, gathering called for tonight. The moment of decision — what the truth is for — arrives at the forge this evening.
The full chain laid out at the Fell Pell — every witness speaking their part, the document roll complete and present. The gathering now sits with the central question: what is the truth for?

Mara named the document roll's destination without being asked — the carrier and route are hers to arrange before the week's end. The accountability chain now has a clear path to the district records office.
Mara named Pip Cotter as carrier — document roll departs in two days for the district records office. Final witness lines open tonight. The accountability chain is nearly sealed.

Silas's own witness statement added to the roll. Fendt named to the fellowship. The gathering sealed with 'Thus begins the fellowship of the Fell.' Document roll now complete — all lines present, ready for Mara to seal and Pip Cotter to carry.

bren-thalle.role: Road opportunist turned toward honest work — miner from lower Wick
bren-thalle.description: A broad man, thirties, worn hands from mining work — grief-carrying and cornered into bad choices by two dry seasons.
history: [d5] Named himself under Huginn's gaze — Bren Thalle, lower Wick settlement, south-slope, three miles past the ford. Dry two seasons. Named the settlement's situation plainly as explana
[d5] Received the hatchet back — handle first, no catch. Tucked it at his belt and started north toward the ford without being asked twice.
knownFacts: From lower Wick settlement, south-slope, three miles past the ford
Miner's settlement, two dry seasons running
This was his first time on the road like this — self-reported, unprompted
Silas returned the hatchet as a tool, not a threat — intent named plainly
Directed to Mara Wells in Millbrook for honest work
bren-thalle.statusNote: Walking north from the limestone bend toward the ford and Millbrook — hatchet at his belt, Curis beside him.

curis.name: Curis
curis.role: Road opportunist turned toward honest work — miner from lower Wick
curis.description: A lean young man of about twenty-two — hollow-eyed from poor eating, grew up in the lower Wick settlement. First time on the road this way, and he volunteered that fact.
history: [d5] Named himself under Huginn's gaze — Curis, same settlement as Bren. Added unprompted that this was the first time. Huginn's shift on the branch was what finally settled him.
[d5] Gathered his provisions and followed Bren north without a word.
knownFacts: From the lower Wick settlement, grew up there
Claimed this was his first time doing this — offered without being asked
curis.statusNote: Walking north from the limestone bend toward the ford and Millbrook — provisions under his arm.

notes: [d2] Silas and Pell crossed the pass on World-day 16 mid-morning — the valley is now behind them. The far side descends into pine run, damp from snowmelt.
[d2] The overhang camp on the far side of the pass holds this night — Pell unguarded, the last of the coals burning low, Huginn attending from the stone lip.

[d4] The junction conduit beneath the inn pulsed once in the third hour of the night — brighter than its normal output, gone before it could be examined. Silas let it pass; it did not read as an ending.
[d4] The bath trough was filled and shared by Silas and Pell on World-day 17 morning — the cistern tap and junction conduit warmth working without trouble.

[d4] The Shadow Work screen was shaped against Calvar's second-floor casement on World-day 18 afternoon — the watcher on the street face has been holding on a covered window since the party's exit.

notes: [d4] Calvar arrived here with the rolled document after extraction from the cooperage — the party occupies the back corner booth. Maret Doss assigned it without being asked.

back-corner-booth.name: Back Corner Booth
back-corner-booth.note: Wall-backed, door in sight-line — the booth Maret Doss assigns to people she has learned not to ask about.

news.text: The Water Crisis stands at Discoloration across the valley: The Echo River runs gray-green on bad mornings. Fish catches are down. Communities murmur but cooperate.

Word reaches you: Millbrook's elder panel formally accepted the evidence of an operating Precursor system beneath the valley — the first official governance body in the valley to name it publicly. (near millbrook)

Word reaches you: Usnea proposed leading a multi-settlement expedition into the Disputed Zone before Millbrook's assembled elder panel — the room could not follow; the gap between evidence and action named openly. (near

Word reaches you: Word of Millbrook's elder decision — and Fendt's public naming — reached the Radiant Plateau Edge District by Day 11 night via the eastern path. Fendt's name appeared in red chalk on the district acco (

Word reaches you: A filtration array supervisor marked their own subordinate — Fendt — on the Edge District accountability board and filed no accompanying record. A keeper of thirty years has never seen this before. (nea

This reaches your area — The lower filtration ledger — containing three weeks of falsified purity readings under Fendt's initials — has been removed from the array-office in the Edge District. (near radiant plateau edge)
Word has spread beyond millbrook: Raised a cleaned rabbit from the dead in the upper meadow — no ritual, no preparation, hands and will and the valley's old grain answering
Word has spread beyond millbrook: Silas Weir bound death-runes into the assembled spear at an open-sky fire, with Pell Marsh's maker's mark seated into the blade and Huginn witness at the stone ring.
Word has spread beyond millbrook: Delivered a dressed bull and sow to Aldric's smokehouse — a significant haul by village standards, carried down from the north wood by three hunters.

Calvar: Reviewing last night's seventh-band log, Calvar found a seventeen-minute gap in the low-frequency baseline that his specification explicitly flags as instrument drift — but the gap's edges are too clean, too symmetrical, and he has spent the morning re-running the check three times rather than recording it as error.

Vash: While dressing her wheel's edge before first light, Vash reconstructed the overheard phrase word by word and realized the term she couldn't place was a pre-Transition calibration interval — specific enough that whoever said it had read the same foundational documents the array's optical layer was built from.

form: Pale skinned, dark hair, slight framed Necromancer - handsome intensity in the eyes - holding the Deathbound Runic Spear "Memory" - His blacksmith lover woman Pell Marsh at his back, undead Raven on his shoulder - Elder Futhark Runes.

bio.motivation: You took the short way, and the short way was wrong. The air here tastes of metal and the light falls at an angle the sun cannot account for. There is a door in the hillside that was not on any map, and behind you the path you walked in on is no longer there.
bio.story: Their first day in the world was the thin place — it is a symbol before it is a door. read what it means; ask him what it is like. You finished something. Completely. It nearly got you exiled, and you are still not sure they were wrong.

[d5] Calvar briefed on Millbrook's layout, key people, and the water crisis stakes on the first hour of the road south. He asked one precise question — intake sleeve material — and filed the rest without c
[d5] Calvar arrived in Millbrook mid-morning on World-day 18 — he read the drainage cut on the road approach and said nothing. The document roll is intact. The chain is in the valley.
[d5] Evening gathering called at Pell's forge — Dara, Aldric, Fendt, Mara, and Calvar all summoned. The chain is complete and in the valley; the meeting tonight ends one thread and will open others.

[d5] Valley response formally delegated at the Fell Pell on World-day 18 evening: Calvar leads east-channel repair crews, Dara Holt runs logistics, Mara runs resupply and communications, Aldric anchors dow

[d3] Piecework lens-grinder of the Edge District — the one whose wheel was turning before the district woke, and who understands the array's optical layer the way water understands a crack

[d3] Leth confirmed the rear corridor access does not lock from the archive side — Vash could have entered during either night after Calvar left the diagram. Leth has agreed to lead Silas to the corridor d

[d3] The folded page is three days old — written the same morning Calvar left the diagram with Leth, within hours of the corridor conversation. The hand is even, the pressure controlled. Vash was not frigh

[d3] Vash is an eyewitness to the file removal — Grael's runner (cut-cord pack, optics array collector, first and third mornings) took the calibration files from the secondary stack six weeks ago. Vash add

[d3] Vash's testimony relayed to Leth — confirmed: the four characters added to Calvar's diagram are authentication marks from a binding receipt, and the folded page explicitly names Grael's runner (cut-co
[d3] Leth will notify Vash tonight that his folded page reached the right hands — he has been waiting three days for that confirmation.

[d4] [while away] While dressing her wheel's edge before first light, Vash reconstructed the overheard phrase word by word and realized the term she couldn't place was a pre-Transition calibration interval

grael-runner.label: Grael's Runner — File Removal Witness
facts: [d3] A figure with a cut-cord pack who collects from the optics array on the first and third morning of each week — named by Vash as the person who physically removed Calvar's calibration files from the Ed

optics-array-intake-log.label: Optics Array Intake Collection Log
facts: [d3] A live working document kept in the array-office foyer — not archived, not restricted. Records every collector visit to the optics array by name, date, and initials. Leth believes it holds the name of

ashwarden-thread.label: The Ashwarden Thread
facts: [d3] Silas named aloud — to Calvar, Pell, and Huginn — that the Ashwarden work is his own track on the road. Huginn acknowledged it without denying it. The bird has not objected to being called his tie to
[d3] Asked what it sees ending in Silas, Huginn chose one word after three breaths: 'Quiet.' It did not elaborate. The work has begun.

the-low-lamp-inn.label: The Low Lamp Inn
facts: [d3] Tucked into the lower tier of the Edge District where the light-guide streets run closest to the valley floor, the Low Lamp sits over a junction conduit — the g
[d3] Tucked in the lower tier of the Edge District — burns oil rather than photonic light by choice, with a gravity-fed heated bath trough. A place that opted for the older warmth deliberately.

[d4] The junction conduit beneath the inn pulsed once in the third hour of the night — brighter than its normal output, momentary. The Attended End registered nothing. Whatever it is, it is not ending.

memory-shared-thread.label: Memory's Shared Thread
facts: [d3] The collar rune on Memory carries a live resonance channel between Silas and Pell — reachable through Order-Sense and Deathsense in combination. At conserved intensity it transmits presence, not words

the-attended-end.label: The Attended End
facts: [d3] The Attended End can anchor a maker's mark — a permanent grain-signature sent as offer rather than claim — through Memory's rune-threshold. The craft stirs and holds it when something acquires permane

[d5] The Attended End read both men at the limestone bend: no death in either frame. The broad man carries a bruised rib and will bury a sister come summer — the aggression is borrowed from grief not yet a
[d5] Named a man's coming grief — a sister, come summer — aloud in front of him, and he knew without asking how the Ashwarden knew. The reading, shown on the face, is its own argument.

maret-doss.label: Maret Doss
facts: [d4] Innkeeper of the Low Lamp Inn — keeps the ledger without ceremony, reads guests fast, offers practical road-knowledge without being asked. Confirmed Silas's Weir mark settled at check-in. Knows the Mi

dav-cutter.label: Dav Cutter
facts: [d4] Cut-cord relay watcher for Grael's network — posted to the south cut-lane behind the cooperage barrel-yard, reading arrivals and passing word

the-ended-threat-wildcraft-braid.label: Ended Threat + Wildcraft: Braided Sequence-Break
facts: [d4] The Ended Threat collapsed the relay figure's forming action at its hinge — the whistle never left the pocket. Wildcraft then placed his attention south with thrown gravel. Used in sequence, the two c

edge-district-interference.label: Edge District — Craft Interference
facts: [d5] The Edge District's photonic order and layered mineral contamination in its groundwater interfere with death-reading crafts — The Attended End returned static noise rather than a clean read. The place

lower-wick-settlement.label: Lower Wick Settlement
facts: [d5] A miner's settlement on the south slope, three miles past the lower ford. Two dry seasons running — the dryness pushed at least two men onto the road. Settlement may have direct knowledge of upstream
[d5] Bren Thalle and Curis directed north to Mara Wells in Millbrook — two of the settlement's displaced heading toward honest work rather than the road.

quiet-ending-in-silas.label: Quiet's Ending
facts: [d5] Huginn named Quiet as ending in Silas. Pell's operational read: three new things since it began — the resurrection in the upper meadow, the spear-binding, the soul-mark exchange. One of these (or thei

quiet-ending.label: Quiet — Structural Read
facts: [d5] Order-Sense turned inward: Quiet was predicated on structural solitude — the single-point standing at the boundary. Three change-points loosened it: the resurrection (violation of ordinary sequence re
[d5] Pell's read: Quiet's ending is not a sickness or a loss — something grew past what it was built for. Three change-points named. The trajectory beyond the loosening has not yet been spoken aloud.
[d5] Pell tested 'Silas Fell Weir' aloud and named it kept — a word for careful use. The middle name is now a settled fact between them, spoken once in full at the forge.
[d5] Silas said 'Fell' back to Pell in his own voice — the middle name is sealed. The three change-points are named and held; the trajectory past the loosening remains unspoken.

silas-ran-weir.label: The Name Ran
facts: [d5] Pell gave Silas his middle name — Ran — on World-day 18 evening. She read it in the spear-binding, from her hand on Memory's grain. Silas Ran Weir.

fell-pell-fellowship.label: The Fellowship of the Fell
facts: [d5] Sealed on World-day 18 evening at the Fell Pell — Silas Weir, Pell, Calvar, Dara Holt, Mara Wells, Aldric, and Fendt. Named aloud: 'Thus begins the fellowship of the Fell.' The deathwork closing was p

dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.name: Dara Holt, the Ditch-Mother
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.role: Senior irrigator responsible for the valley's eastern channel network
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.voiceHints: Broad-shouldered, mid-fifties, hands that look like they were carved from the same hardwood as a shovel handle. Speaks in measurements and seasons — 'that valve's been weeping since last thaw,' not 'something's wrong.' Never dramatizes. Brings a cracked fitting to prove the point before she finishes the sentence. Calls a problem by its part name, not by its consequence.
knowledge: Forty years of the eastern channels — every slope, silting pattern, and freeze-line in the network

The river's mineral load has shifted since late autumn: fittings that lasted a decade are threading out or snapping in under two seasons, and the corrosion pattern runs inward from the joint, not outward the way sediment wear does
Three other irrigators on the eastern bench are seeing the same losses but haven't connected them yet — she has
The stretch of river above Holt's intake smells faintly wrong in still mornings, a faint bitterness she can't name but recognizes as new
She keeps a tin of failed fittings sorted by failure date and location — a physical record no one asked her to keep
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.wants: Replacement valve stock she can trust, a forge-backed answer for why her iron is failing, and confirmation that the river is the cause — not her installation work. She is not here to be reassured; she is here for a diagnosis.
dara-holt-the-ditch-mother.fears: That the river is doing something the valley has never budgeted for and that this spring's planting window will close before anyone in authority admits it.

kit.reveal: Brings physical evidence — dated, sorted, labeled — that makes the river's pattern undeniable to anyone willing to look at the parts
kit.bind: Connects isolated reports from neighboring irrigators into a coherent problem, naming the network's shared vulnerability before it fragments into blame

reactsToReputation.competent-and-direct: opens her tin of failed fittings immediately, shares dates and locations without being asked
reactsToReputation.dismissive-or-theoretical: polite, brief, and gone — she'll find someone who works with their hands
reactsToReputation.curious-and-honest: stays longer than she planned, starts drawing channel maps on whatever surface is available

provenance.hint: A broad-shouldered farmer, older than Pell, with cracked irrigation tubing and a practical manner — has been watching th
provenance.why: She arrived at the forge door with a named problem (brittle threading, cracked valve) and direct knowledge of what the r

bette-harrow-the-seed-ledger.name: Bette Harrow, the Seed Ledger
bette-harrow-the-seed-ledger.role: Contract seed-agent for the upvalley growers' cooperative, collecting outstanding grain-debts before the expedition locks up the forge accounts
bette-harrow-the-seed-ledger.voiceHints: Mid-forties, weathered apron, ink-stained fingers. Speaks in numbered points without meaning to — 'First. Second. Done.' Knock was three times, flat and evenly spaced, the knock of someone who has knocked on a hundred doors this season and will knock on a hundred more. Carries a wax-sealed ledger book under one arm and smells faintly of thaw-mud and linseed. Does not waste warmth, but is not cold — simply occupied.
knowledge: Pell's forge owes the cooperative fourteen measures of worked iron against last autumn's seed-credit — the debt was rolled once already and the cooperative cannot roll it again before spring planting
Three other valley craftsmen settled accounts this week; Pell is the last open line
She heard at the mill this morning that the forge party is departing today — that is exactly why she knocked before seventh bell
The cooperative's eastern field irrigation channel needs a new sluice plate; she is authorized to negotiate that ironwork as partial settlement if Pell is short on coin
She knows Corvin Aldric by name and reputation and was relieved it was neither of them who answered — she finds them harder to settle with than a working smith
bette-harrow-the-seed-ledger.wants: Close the account today, in full or in trade, before the expedition leaves and the debt floats until midsummer. She has fourteen other stops before noon and the mud on the south lane is already soft.
bette-harrow-the-seed-ledger.fears: That the forge will be shuttered for weeks mid-season and the cooperative will have to explain to the upvalley families why planting is delayed because of an uncollected iron-debt — her name is on that ledger too.

reactsToReputation.honest-dealer: softens immediately, willing to negotiate trade-in-kind with a handshake and no witness
reactsToReputation.debt-dodger: produces the ledger, reads the clause aloud, does not raise her voice, does not leave

kit.expression: The ledger itself is the instrument — every entry dated, witnessed, and cross-referenced to the cooperative's master roll. She can produce the binding obligation in writing on the spot (bind), and if a party disputes a figure she can walk the arithmetic aloud, line by line, until the error or the truth surfaces (reveal). No magic. Just ink, memory, and early-spring light through a forge doorway.

arrivalDetail.timing: World-day 15, just after first light, while frost is still on the south-lane cobbles
arrivalDetail.knock: Three knocks — brisk, flat, evenly spaced. Business knock. Not a friend's knock.
arrivalDetail.openingWords: "Bette Harrow, upvalley cooperative. I need three minutes with whoever holds the forge accounts. I know the hour."

provenance.hint: an unknown visitor at the south lane of Pell's forge — brisk knock, not Corvin or Aldric, someone with business or urgen
provenance.why: three knocks at the forge door with no established identity — the world pressing in on the expedition's departure

pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.name: Pip Cotter, Mara's Errand-Runner
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.role: Child courier and small-task helper for Mara Wells
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.appearance: Seven years old, possibly eight — she is unsure and will tell you both ages depending on which she remembers. Wears an oversized blue wool coat that was someone else's first, the cuffs rolled four times and still dragging. Cheeks red from the cold and the running. Clutches whatever she's carrying in both hands like it is the most important object in the world, because Mara said so.
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.voiceHints: Breathless sentences, pauses to swallow air mid-thought. Repeats Mara's instructions almost verbatim before adding her own opinions uninvited. Calls adults by full name because that is what she has heard done. Takes promises seriously and remembers them exactly.

kit.move: Knows every shortcut between Millbrook's lanes and garden gates — the gap in the Cotter fence, the stepping-stones behind the mill, the path along the east ditch that stays dry into spring.
kit.reveal: Absorbs overheard details without meaning to and repeats them with complete accuracy when she trusts the listener, or when she forgets to be careful.

knowledge: The contents of the cloth Mara sent — she memorized Mara's words about it before she left, in case the cloth got wet
Every house in Millbrook that keeps a dog and which ones bite
That Mara has been up before dawn three mornings running — Pip noticed because she was also up, feeding the goat
Which neighbor children are sick this season and which are not — valley gossip absorbed from doorstep conversations while waiting to be let in
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.wants: To complete the errand correctly, catch her breath, and be given a small task on the return trip so the journey feels useful twice.
pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.fears: Losing or ruining something she was trusted to carry. Being told she arrived too late.

reactsToReputation.trusted-by-mara: opens up immediately, will answer two questions for every one asked
reactsToReputation.unkind-to-children: goes quiet, delivers the message in the fewest possible words, leaves fast

pip-cotter-mara-s-errand-runner.notes: Not yet aware that she is a reliable source of low-level local intelligence. Mara is aware. Pip is loyal to Mara first and will report any strange treatment back to her without being asked.

provenance.hint: a girl of seven or eight in an oversized blue wool coat — Mara's errand-runner, practical and out of breath
provenance.why: The child messenger has just arrived in-scene with a cloth from Mara and needs a stable identity for future encounters

calvar.name: Calvar
calvar.role: Pre-Transition filtration engineer, author of the seventh-band monitoring specification — a man who stopped moving fifteen years ago because the light in one room told him everything he still needed to know
calvar.physicalDetail: Late fifties, though early-spring morning light makes him look older — the kind of age that comes from stillness, not hardship. Hands still precise. Keeps the window above the cooperage angled so the first plateau light crosses his drafting surface at exactly the seventh-band frequency. He has calibrated this by hand, yearly, for fifteen years.
calvar.voiceHints: Unhurried in a way that is not rudeness — he simply will not be rushed past the part that matters. Speaks about light the way other engineers speak about load-bearing calculations: not reverently, but exactly. When he senses evasion he goes very quiet and waits, and the waiting is harder than argument. Will not leave a sentence technically imprecise even when it costs him emotionally.

abilities.reveal: Calvar reads spectral signatures in ambient light the way others read handwriting — he can identify filtration drift, contamination band, or a falsified purity reading from the quality of illumination in a room. This is not mysticism; it is fifteen years of sitting still with the seventh band until it became a language.
abilities.bind: The seventh-band specification is a living instrument to him. He knows every node it touches, every engineer who trained under it, every deviation that has ever been logged. Persuading him is not a social act — it is a technical one. Give him a true reading and he is bound to it. Give him a false one and you have lost him entirely.

knowledge: The seventh-band monitoring specification in its entirety, including three unpublished addenda he chose not to file
What the diagram he left with Leth three days ago means — and what it does not mean, which is the more important half
That the seventh-band readings have been drifting in a direction that has no name in the pre-Transition literature, and has had no name for eleven months

The cooperage below him has been storing something other than barrels for two seasons; he has not reported it because the light it emits is not dangerous — it is, in a way he cannot yet specify, attentive
That leaving the room means losing the calibration, and that losing the calibration means the specification becomes a document rather than a living thing
calvar.wants: To be given a reason to leave that is technically sound — not an appeal to safety, not an appeal to duty, but a reading he cannot take from this window. He will go if the data requires it. He will not go for sentiment.
calvar.fears: That the drift in the seventh band is not contamination but communication, and that moving before he understands it will mean the message arrives and no one is left who can read it.

reactsToReputation.truthful: he opens the second notebook, the one he has not shown anyone
reactsToReputation.technically_credible: he treats you as a colleague and the conversation shifts from persuasion to collaboration
reactsToReputation.evasive_or_political: he answers every question accurately and volunteers nothing; the diagram becomes the whole of what you get

calvar.relationshipToLeth: Trusts Leth's judgment about people, not about urgency. The diagram was a gift, not an alarm — he expected Leth to sit with it for a month, not three days.
calvar.currentState: Early spring has shifted the morning angle two degrees north. He has been recalibrating since first light. The seventh-band reading this morning was the clearest it has been in eleven months, and he is not certain whether this means the drift is resolving or peaking.

provenance.hint: Calvar — pre-Transition filtration engineer, late fifties or older, living above the cooperage on the east lane of the E
provenance.why: Named by Leth as the engineer the party must find and persuade to leave the Edge District; the party will go to him next

offscreen.note: Reviewing last night's seventh-band log, Calvar found a seventeen-minute gap in the low-frequency baseline that his specification explicitly flags as instrument drift — but the gap's edges are too clean, too symmetrical, and he has spent the morning re-running the check three times rather than recording it as error.

vash.name: Vash
vash.role: Piecework lens-grinder of the Edge District — the one whose wheel was turning before the district woke, and who understands the array's optical layer the way water understands a crack
vash.voiceHints: Unhurried. Speaks in the cadence of someone who has spent years listening to the pitch of a wheel and knows that rushing ruins the curve. Does not perform ignorance but does not announce knowledge either — waits to see whether you are asking the real question or the surface one. When she decides you mean it, she will tell you exactly what she heard, what she saw, and what she made of it, in that order, without decoration.
knowledge: The fresher notation on the diagram — she heard Calvar speak it aloud, half to himself, while waiting for Leth's door; she did not know what it meant then and has been turning it over since
The optical layer of the array at a depth the weavers who commission her work do not suspect: she grinds to tolerances they specify without understanding why, and she has come to understand why
Which lenses in the eastern relay cluster were reground on non-standard prescription, and whose chit authorized it — she kept the chit because the prescription was wrong in an interesting way
That Leth's archive door carries sound badly, and that the east room is the worst-kept secret on the row
vash.wants: To understand what she overheard well enough to know whether she should have said something sooner. She is not afraid of consequence — she is afraid of having held a piece of something important and not known what it was a piece of.
vash.fears: Being made into a witness for something she only half-understood. That the half she missed is the half that matters.
vash.physicalDetail: Fine-grained abrasive dust has settled permanently into the creases of her hands and the inner corners of her eyes. In early-spring light, standing near a window, she looks briefly like she is made of the same material she works.
vash.dailyPattern: At the wheel before first light. Done by mid-morning. Afternoons she is difficult to find — she walks the valley rim, which the district assumes is idleness and which is not.

reactsToReputation.curious: she meets curiosity with curiosity; will ask what you already know before she adds to it
reactsToReputation.authoritative: goes quiet and literal; answers only exactly what is asked, nothing adjacent
reactsToReputation.truthful: opens — will produce the chit; will tell you what she heard word for word including her uncertainty about one syllable
reactsToReputation.threatening: does not flinch, does not flee; looks at you with the patience of someone who has learned that pressure applied wrong ruins the work

provenance.hint: Vash — a lens-grinder in the Edge District, east room of the same row as Leth's archive, does piecework for the optics w
provenance.why: Leth has just named Vash as the person who was on the grinding wheel the morning Calvar came — potentially the overhear

offscreen.note: While dressing her wheel's edge before first light, Vash reconstructed the overheard phrase word by word and realized the term she couldn't place was a pre-Transition calibration interval — specific enough that whoever said it had read the same foundational documents the array's optical layer was built from.

dav-cutter.name: Dav Cutter
dav-cutter.role: Cut-cord relay watcher for Grael's network — posted to the south cut-lane behind the cooperage barrel-yard, reading arrivals and passing word
dav-cutter.voiceHints: Young enough that his stillness costs him something — he practices it. Speaks rarely and in pieces: a half-sentence, a direction of the chin, a breath held too long. Valley-grain runs through him despite himself; he reads the quality of silence the way a lay-brother reads scripture, and he knows when a light has changed before he can say how. Hand never leaves the left lapel of his coat. When Silas came through the gate, Dav felt it like a shifted candle-flame — and has not yet decided what the feeling means.
knowledge: The relay hand-signs Grael's cutcords use along the south cooperage lane — three variations for stranger, known, and known-and-wrong
The rhythm of the barrel-yard: which hours the coopers go quiet, where the gate shadow falls in early-spring morning light
That Silas came through — the shape of his arrival, not yet its meaning
The Low Lamp's back-passage geography well enough to be gone before a question becomes a confrontation
That the valley's stillness is not emptiness — it is attention, and he has learned, uneasily, to trust that
dav-cutter.wants: To pass the word clean and be done — no interpretation, no consequence, just the relay completed and the coin earned. He is quietly afraid that this particular message will not let him stay that simple.
dav-cutter.fears: That the valley's light will eventually make him legible — that something in the stillness here will read him back the way he reads arrivals, and that what it finds will cost him the cord.

reactsToReputation.network_known: relaxes one degree, does not show it, confirms the relay sign
reactsToReputation.outsider: goes mannequin-still, waits for the person to leave his lane
reactsToReputation.spiritually_attuned: unsettles him; he does not know why they keep looking slightly past him, and it makes him want to move
reactsToReputation.threatening: already gone — he was never quite here

abilities.name: Cut-Lane Read
abilities.description: Dav processes arrivals through the gate against the quality of early-spring morning light — the angle of a shadow, a breath misting wrong, a coat worn heavy on one side. He does not know he is doing it spiritually. He thinks it is just attention.

abilities.name: Relay Sign
abilities.description: Passes encoded hand-signal up the network without voice or paper — stranger, known, or known-and-wrong. The message moves; he stays. The valley stillness carries it faster than it should.

abilities.name: Cord-Cut Fade
abilities.description: Dav can vacate a lane so cleanly that witnesses reconstruct him out of ambient detail — a barrel stave, a shadow, the smell of new wood. In the valley's strong light, this costs him more than it used to. The light keeps finding edges.

provenance.hint: A relay watcher — young, cut-cord pack, hand near his coat, Grael's network — standing in the cut-lane south of the coop
provenance.why: A second watcher posted to cover the cooperage's rear exit, distinct from the fixed watcher on the street face — the par

the-low-lamp-inn.name: The Low Lamp Inn
the-low-lamp-inn.descriptionSeed: Tucked into the lower tier of the Edge District where the light-guide streets run closest to the valley floor, the Low Lamp sits over a junction conduit — the glow rises through pale stone floors and fills the common room from below, warm and directionless as afternoon through cloud. The building is two storeys of dressed sandstone with deep-set windows fitted with diffusion panels so the light inside is never harsh. The beds are thick-mattressed, the bath-room fed by a cistern the owner keeps properly heated, and the linens smell of cedar oil. Maret Doss, who runs it, is a compact woman of sixty-odd years whose conversational range does not include your name, your business, where you've come from, or where you're going. She does ask whether you'd prefer the bath before or after supper, because the water pressure is better before the evening crowd. She has been here since before the last treaty renegotiation and expects to be here after the next. The common room is quiet enough to think in, which is unusual for the Edge District and not accidental.
the-low-lamp-inn.encounterFlavor: A common room lit gold-from-below where two or three guests eat separately and don't introduce themselves; early-spring rain ticking against diffusion panels; the smell of cedar, hot water, and whatever Maret is cooking that isn't your concern unless you'd like a bowl. Occasionally a figure comes in from the cold, exchanges a look with Maret, goes upstairs, and is not there in the morning.
questSeeds: A guest in the corner room has been here eleven nights and hasn't come down today — Maret won't say how she knows something is wrong, only that she'd like it handled quietly and without the District wardens

The light-guide conduit under the inn has been cycling strangely at the third hour of the night, waking guests with a pulse of light brighter than it should be — someone with knowledge of Radiant infrastructure could trace it, if they were careful about who found out they were looking

A sealed letter was left with Maret for a guest who checked in under a name that matches someone Silas or Pell have reason to recognize — Maret will not confirm or deny the guest exists, but she hasn't burned the letter

Maret's cedar oil supplier hasn't come up from the valley floor in three weeks; she mentions this once, flatly, and doesn't mention it again — but the route he travels crosses ground that has been quietly problematic

provenance.hint: a modest but genuinely comfortable inn in the Edge District of the Radiant Plateau — warm, lit from below by the light-g
provenance.why: Silas and Pell need a named, returnable inn in the Edge District to spend the night — it will be a place they may return

the-attended-end.name: The Attended End
the-attended-end.description: Marrow tells you plainly what is ending here and how long it has — a person, a truce, a building, a road. It is never wrong and it never softens it.
the-attended-end.notFor: Preventing the ending; being asked to be gentle about it; anything that is not, in fact, ending.
the-attended-end.narrationHints: Marrow tells you plainly what is ending here and how long it has — a person, a truce, a building, a road. It is never wrong and it never softens it.

corrections.why: SNG-068/085 — creation committed before confirm: the prologue derived his domains and abilities from paths he played, not from the character he chose. He is a NECROMANCER; the game made him a wright-domain character with Blazeborn/Lattice/Numinous abilities, and his breadth cap (3 at level 2) was fully spent on them, locking him out of his own people's crafts entirely.

changes.note: the death pole. ORIGIN stays 'wright' — he is a Wright who FINISHED something and was nearly exiled for it, and now practices the craft of ending. That contradiction is the best thing about him and it is not a bug.

NET ZERO — 3 before, 3 after. A repair, not an advance. order_sense KEPT at Erik's request; it is 4 steps from his secondary (penalized, legal) and it buys THE MEASURED RUIN (wither + order_sense): 'decay applied with a designer's precision — this beam and not that one'. That is a smart necromancer.

corrections.notGranted: No xp, no levels, no skill points, no items. His 1 skill point is untouched — he spends it himself.

corrections.from: Pale slight framed Necromancer - handsome intensity in the eyes and the hold of the clutching hand - green/blue power emanating from him with his undead Raven on his shoulder.
corrections.to: Pale skinned, dark hair, slight framed Necromancer - handsome intensity in the eyes and the hold of the clutching hand - green/blue power emanating from him with his undead Raven on his shoulder.

chronicleCache.hash: d5|bpell:romantic:together|c|q|L10|You took the short way, and the short way was wr
chronicleCache.text: You have become the man who took the short way and found it was the only way — the path back gone, the light wrong, and something in you already changed before you understood what had changed you. You talked Calvar out of the Edge District, pulled him away from his filtration schematics and his seventh-band specifications and set him walking toward Millbrook to say what he knew about Grael's reign, and you did the same with Fendt, who carried his testimony in his own hands, the falsified ledger thread no longer just evidence but a wound walking upright. You fed a village — a dressed bull and sow carried down from the north wood — and you raised a cleaned rabbit in the upper meadow with no ritual and no preparation, just your hands and whatever the valley keeps in its old grain, which answered. At Pell Marsh's forge you named the death-bound spear Memory at dawn after a night of wine and repair, her maker's mark seated into the blade and Huginn watching at the stone ring; you kissed her at the forge bench while the light was still coming on, and that word, that deed, that mouth — all of it holds. You are bound now: to Pell, to Calvar who trusted you, to Aldric and Fendt and the rest of them; the spear carries your death-runes and a name that stuck, and wherever the hillside door leads, you are carrying all of that through it.

sessions.recap: Day seventeen passed through Silas Weir like weather through an open window — present, felt, and gone without leaving a mark anyone could name. No blood on his hands, no new doors opened or closed, no word carried to ears that needed it. He existed inside the hours, which is its own kind of labor, the kind that leaves no scar but slowly shapes the bone beneath. The world held its breath, and Silas held his alongside it.

gm-road-opportunists-at-the-hawth.name: Road Opportunists at the Hawthorn Bend
gm-road-opportunists-at-the-hawth.setup: Two tired road-workers turned brigands have boxed the party at a narrow limestone bend — the broad one with a hatchet and the lean one with an iron-shod crook, both close enough that the ground forecloses easy retreat. Huginn is already above them.
